# UC-22.52.4 · SOCI s 30AG board approval evidence — every CIRMP and annual report has a signed board minute

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We make sure the board has formally signed off on the written risk plan, and on the annual report sent to the regulator. If a plan is in use without a signature on file, or the annual report is about to go out without a board nod, we ring the bell so the Company Secretary can chase it down.*

---

## Description

For every CIRMP version in force AND for the current year's annual report, verifies a signed board-approval minute artefact exists with named signatory, approval date, and a link to the signed minute. Surfaces missing approvals and missing artefacts separately so the Company Secretary can chase the right step.

## Value

Without board approval the CIRMP has no legal weight under s 30AG. The CISC will ask: 'Show me the board minute approving the current CIRMP and the annual report.' This UC keeps that artefact register always-on; without it, the Company Secretary spends days at audit time chasing PDFs across drives.

## Implementation

(1) Build the board-portal webhook integration: every board resolution is HECd to `index=board_secretariat` with `resolution_type` (`cirmp_approval`, `cirmp_annual_report`, etc.). (2) Materialise `cirmp_board_approval_lookup` from `cirmp_approval` resolutions, keyed by (asset_id, cirmp_version), with the signed minute URL. (3) Schedule UC-22.52.4 daily; route `approval_status=missing_board_approval` to notable (severity critical) and to Company Secretary email. (4) Archive all rows to `audit_evidence`.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.52.4` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-s30AG"`. Every row pins (asset_id, cirmp_version, board_approved_at, board_minute_url, signatory, signatory_role, annual_report_year, annual_report_approved_at, annual_report_minute_url). Dashboard `SOCI — CIRMP Board Approval Register` lists every CIRMP version and annual report with linked minute artefacts, surfaces any CIRMP without a corresponding board approval, and exports an attestation pack for the Rule 10 annual report.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Publish a new CIRMP version in the GRC system but DO NOT record a corresponding board-approval minute. Within 24 hours UC-22.52.4 MUST flag the CIRMP with `approval_status="missing_board_approval"`, route a notable to the Company Secretary, and prevent the CIRMP version from being marked 'in force' by the GRC workflow.

### Negative scenario

Publish a new CIRMP version AND record a corresponding board-approval minute (board_approved_at, signed_minute_url, signatory). UC-22.52.4 MUST record `approval_status="approved"` and MUST NOT raise an alert.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.52.2 deployed (provides `cirmp_catalogue_lookup`) and UC-22.52.3 deployed (provides `cirmp_review_lookup`).
- Board-portal webhook integration. Most modern board portals (Diligent Boards, OnBoard, Convene) emit a resolution-passed webhook; subscribe with a long-lived secret stored in a vault.
- HEC token configured for `index=board_secretariat sourcetype=board:portal:resolution`.
- `index=board_secretariat` (10-year retention online — board minutes are corporate records).
- `index=audit_evidence` (7-year retention online).
- Lookups:
  - `cirmp_catalogue_lookup` (shared with UC-22.52.2).
  - `cirmp_board_approval_lookup` — owned by Board Secretariat. Columns: `asset_id`, `cirmp_version`, `board_approved_at` (epoch), `signatory` (name), `signatory_role` (Chair / Director / Accountable Officer), `minute_url` (link to signed PDF in board portal).
- ES notable framework configured with `audit` security domain.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

### Board-portal webhook

Most modern board portals support outbound webhooks for resolution-passed events. Subscribe the webhook to a Splunk HEC endpoint behind an authentication layer. Example payload (Diligent Boards-style):

```json
{
  "resolution_id": "DLG-2026-0142",
  "resolution_type": "cirmp_approval",
  "asset_id": "AU-NEM-GEN-001",
  "cirmp_version": "4.2",
  "approved_at": "2026-05-13T11:00:00+10:00",
  "signatory": "J. Smith",
  "signatory_role": "Chair of the Board",
  "signed_minute_url": "https://boards.responsible-entity.local/minutes/DLG-2026-0142"
}
```

A small Splunk-edge proxy (or Splunk Connect for HTTP) normalises this into `sourcetype=board:portal:resolution` and HECs to `index=board_secretariat`.

Then materialise the lookup nightly:

```spl
index=board_secretariat sourcetype=board:portal:resolution resolution_type=cirmp_approval earliest=-5y@y
| dedup asset_id cirmp_version sortby - _time
| rename _time AS board_approved_at, signed_minute_url AS minute_url
| table asset_id cirmp_version board_approved_at signatory signatory_role minute_url
| outputlookup cirmp_board_approval_lookup
```

Validate:

```spl
index=board_secretariat earliest=-12mon
| stats count by resolution_type
```

Expect to see `cirmp_approval`, `cirmp_annual_report`, `policy_approval` types. The presence of `cirmp_approval` confirms the webhook is wired.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search is two left-joins: one against `cirmp_board_approval_lookup` for the per-version approval, and one against `index=board_secretariat` for the annual-report approval for the current year.

Schedule:

```ini
[UC-22.52.4: SOCI s 30AG board approval evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.4,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-s30AG
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI s 30AG CIRMP missing board approval
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = critical
alert.severity = 5
```

Notable creation on `approval_status IN (missing_board_approval, approved_no_artefact)` or `annual_report_status=missing_annual_report_approval`. Archive every row.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Webhook health**:

```spl
index=board_secretariat sourcetype=board:portal:resolution earliest=-30d
| timechart span=1d count
```

Flat-zero during business days suggests the webhook is broken or the secret has expired.

**(b) Coverage**:

```spl
| inputlookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup
| dedup asset_id cirmp_version
| lookup cirmp_board_approval_lookup asset_id cirmp_version OUTPUT board_approved_at
| stats count(eval(isnull(board_approved_at))) AS missing count AS total
```

Gap > 0 is a P1 finding.

**(c) Positive control**: publish a new CIRMP version in the GRC sandbox without registering a board approval. Next-day UC must flag with `approval_status=missing_board_approval`.

**(d) Negative control**: publish a new version AND emit a corresponding board-portal webhook. Next-day UC must record `approval_status=approved`.

**(e) Quarterly Company-Secretary attestation**: the Company Secretary signs a quarterly attestation that the approval register is complete; filed in `audit_evidence`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — CIRMP Board Approval Register`.

- **Panel 1, RAG table**: in-force CIRMP versions per asset, with approval status, signatory, minute URL.
- **Panel 2, sortable table**: every CIRMP version with missing or artefact-incomplete approvals — the Company Secretary's chase list.
- **Panel 3, annual-report approval status**: per asset and year, with drill-through to the resolution.
- **Panel 4, 5-year timeline**: every approval event — a longitudinal evidence base for the CIRMP annual report.
- **Panel 5, signatory mix**: which directors signed off on which CIRMPs — supports board-rotation considerations.

**Runbook for `approval_status=missing_board_approval`** (owner: Company Secretary, severity P1):

1. Within 5 business days: place the CIRMP on the next board agenda; if the next board meeting is >30 days away, organise a flying minute (circular resolution).
2. Record the approval as soon as it is granted; the webhook should auto-populate the lookup.
3. Withdraw the 'in force' status in the GRC system until approval is recorded — prevents the CIRMP catalogue from showing an unapproved CIRMP as authoritative.
4. Self-report any operating period without approval in the next Rule 10 annual report.

**Runbook for `annual_report_status=missing_annual_report_approval`** (owner: Company Secretary, severity P1):

1. The annual report is due to the Department within 90 days of financial-year end. Without board approval the report cannot be submitted under s 30AG.
2. Calendar the approval into the board meeting that falls within 60 days of financial-year end.
3. Escalate to the Chair if the meeting slips.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Webhook secret expired** — the board portal silently stops emitting; coverage panel goes red. Rotate the secret and replay the missed events via the board-portal admin console.

**Signatory role ambiguous** — the portal sends 'Director' but the lookup expects 'Chair'. Normalise in the modular input field-mapping.

**One CIRMP shows two approvals** — amendments or re-approvals. `dedup` by `latest(_time)`.

**Annual-report approval looks missing but a verbal approval was granted** — the SOCI Act requires evidence; verbal approval is insufficient. Insist on a signed resolution.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Parent-board approval delegation**: an ASX-listed entity with a foreign parent may have approval delegated. Document the delegation chain in the lookup and supply both signatories.
- **Joint-venture assets**: an asset jointly owned by multiple responsible entities requires board approval from each. Lookup must surface every responsible entity's approval.
- **Government-shareholder approval**: state-owned entities may have shareholder-Minister approval as the equivalent of board approval; treat the Treasurer's letter as the artefact.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup
| dedup asset_id cirmp_version
| lookup cirmp_board_approval_lookup asset_id cirmp_version OUTPUT board_approved_at signatory signatory_role minute_url
| eval approval_status = case(
    isnotnull(board_approved_at) AND isnotnull(minute_url) AND isnotnull(signatory), "approved",
    isnotnull(board_approved_at) AND isnull(minute_url), "approved_no_artefact",
    1=1, "missing_board_approval")
| eval annual_report_year = strftime(now(), "%Y")
| join type=left asset_id annual_report_year [
    search index=board_secretariat sourcetype="board:portal:resolution" earliest=-1y@y resolution_type="cirmp_annual_report"
    | rename _time AS annual_report_approved_at, signed_minute_url AS annual_report_minute_url
    | eval annual_report_year = strftime(_time, "%Y")
    | fields asset_id annual_report_year annual_report_approved_at annual_report_minute_url
]
| eval annual_report_status = if(isnotnull(annual_report_approved_at), "approved", "missing_annual_report_approval")
| table asset_id cirmp_version approval_status board_approved_at signatory signatory_role minute_url annual_report_year annual_report_status annual_report_approved_at annual_report_minute_url
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) RAG table: every in-force CIRMP version with approval status, signatory, and minute URL. (Panel 2) Sortable table: every CIRMP version missing board approval. (Panel 3) Annual-report board-approval status by asset and year. (Panel 4) Timeline of approvals across the last 5 years — establishes a longitudinal evidence base.

## Known False Positives

1. **Out-of-cycle board sub-committee approval** — some entities delegate CIRMP approval to a board risk sub-committee with a different resolution type. Suppression: extend the `resolution_type` enumeration in the webhook integration and the lookup.

2. **Approval in a foreign jurisdiction** — ASX-listed entities with overseas parent boards may have approval at the parent-board level. Suppression: support a `parent_board_approval_artefact` field in the lookup.

3. **Accountable-officer signature (no board)** — single-shareholder or government-owned entities may have an accountable officer instead of a board. Suppression: treat `signatory_role IN ("Accountable Officer", "Secretary of the Department", "Chief Executive")` as equivalent.

4. **Minute artefact in a different store** — some entities store minutes in SharePoint not the board portal. Suppression: extend the webhook integration or surface a manual lookup row for those entities.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_4_exceptions` with Company Secretary sign-off; max grant 30 days because board-approval gap is high-risk.

## References

- [Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018 (Cth) — s 30AG](https://www.legislation.gov.au/C2018A00029/latest/text)
- [CISC — CIRMP annual report guidance](https://www.cisc.gov.au/legislation-regulation-and-compliance/critical-infrastructure-risk-management-program)
